title: Workhorse 2024 Earnings Reveal Major Transformation: New Models, Lower Losses
text: Workhorse 2024 earnings reveal major transformation with new models and lower losses despite revenue challenges. The company reported $6.6M in sales, down from $13.1M in 2023, with net loss of $101.8M improved from $123.9M in 2023. The sales decrease was primarily attributed to lower W4 CC truck sales compared with the same period a year ago. The company launched six models in production with four additional models planned for 2025. Workhorse achieved significant cost reductions through employee cost reductions of $3M, inventory savings of $4.4M, consulting expense cuts of $1.5M, and freight expense reductions of $1.4M. Transport Canada approved W4 CC and W56 models for the Canadian market with demo truck deployment planned for April 2025. The company secured a GSA contract and expanded its cooperative purchasing agreements including Sourcewell, Florida Sheriff's Association Purchasing Program, OMNIA Partners, and PASSPort.
